documents.title: Raynor
documents.subtitle: Renegade Commander
documents.text:
Raynor has access to a mix of classical Terran Biological units (such as Marines and Marauders) and Mechanical Units (such as Siege Tanks and Vikings). He can drop units directly at their rally points. Combined with his potentially limitless access to MULEs, strong macro capabilities will be rewarded.

He is generally a difficult commander to play, and even harder to master. But at high levels of play, he is able to spawn-camp ground attack waves and sustain pushes indefinitely.

documents.title: Kerrigan
documents.subtitle: Queen of Blades
documents.text:
Kerrigan plays with classical Zerg units such as Zerglings, Hydralisks and Mutalisks. Her mobility is greatly improved by the availability of her Omega Worms. However, her greatest strength is the Kerrigan hero unit herself. Kerrigan has access to a wide range of abilities that provide the player with a strong early-game presence, while simultaneously offering them the capability of improving their economy.

Kerrigan's is an easy commander to play, but does require good micro of the hero unit to truly excel in games.

documents.title: Artanis
documents.subtitle: Hierarch of the Daelaam
documents.text:
Artanis uses classic Protoss units as the main components of his army. While his army units themselves are fairly fragile, his Spear of Adun abilities compensate for his army's weaknesses by providing him with abilities that increase their survivability.

Artanis is a commander with simple mechanics, but a player will have to be attentive so as to keep his army alive. With adequate understanding of enemy compositions, a player can use Artanis' army to control entire attack waves, or weaken them using his Spear of Adun abilities.

documents.title: Swann
documents.subtitle: Chief Engineer
documents.text:
Swann utilizes Terran Mechanical units such as Siege Tanks and Thors to build up his army. These units are backed up by his powerful Drakken Laser Drill, which can be upgraded to unlock additional abilities. Finally, he is also able to construct a wide variety of static defense, allowing him to control strategic positions on the map.

Swann is a very difficult commander to play, and requires a lot of micro to ensure his army remains effective. However, a veteran Swann player's army can be terrifyingly strong, shredding any attack wave that approaches it within seconds.

documents.title: Zagara
documents.subtitle: Swarm Broodmother
documents.text:
Zagara's standard army composition includes a mix of Zerglings, Baneling and Scourge. With the help of the Frenzy buff that Zagara's hero unit herself provides, a maximum supply army rarely struggles against any enemy composition or any enemy base. She is a hyper-aggressive commander, but will struggle on certain defensive missions.

Zagara is a fairly easy commander to play that favors a more macro-centric playstyle. While at low levels, Zagara is a very weak commander, at Ascension levels, she is one of the most powerful commanders available if played right, with a very strong early game presence.

documents.title: Vorazun
documents.subtitle: Matriarch of the Nerazim
documents.text:
Vorazun provides passive buffs to cloaked units that increase their shield and energy regeneration. Her army is comprised of high damage, low HP units, and thus, will require attention on the player's behalf. However, Vorazun dominates in terms of crowd control and disabling enemies with some of the most powerful abilities in co-op.

Vorazun is a simple commander to play. Players that have good map awareness and are efficient with their calldowns will have no problems handling anything thrown at them.

documents.title: Karax
documents.subtitle: Khalai Phase-Smith
documents.text:
Karax specializes in the usage of Protoss Mechanical units and static defense. His playstyle requires good map awareness, efficient use of static defense, as well as careful usage of his Spear of Adun abilities.

Karax is one of the hardest commanders to play correctly. Significantly weak during sub-mastery and sub-ascension play, extra care must be taken so as to not lose his army while taking engagements.

documents.title: Abathur
documents.subtitle: Evolution Master
documents.text:
Abathur's units, which consists of classic Zerg units such as Roaches and Mutalisks, collect Biomass from slain enemies. With enough Biomass, they evolve into powerful Ultimate Evolutions that can essentially complete missions without any further backup. His first Ultimate Evolution provides him with extreme early-game capabilities.

Abathur's difficulty depends on the initiative a player takes, generally rewarding aggressive play. Farming Biomass in the early game leads to a very straightforward late-game, including spawn-camping ground attack waves and complete map control.

documents.title: Alarak
documents.subtitle: Highlord of the Tal'darim
documents.text:
Alarak's Tal'darim Ascendants have some of the highest damage output of any units in the game. However, these come at a cost of being extremely difficult to build up. As a result, Alarak relies on his Hero unit as well as his Photon Overload ability to provide him with the damage output to deal with the early stages of the game. He excels at dealing damage to units, but does struggle a bit when it comes to clearing structures.

Alarak is one of the most difficult commanders to play well. He is extremely punishing if a player takes a bad engagement, almost finding it impossible to recover. But with adequate understanding of his mechanics, Alarak players can decimate enemy waves and melt through attack waves while taking minimal losses.

documents.title: Nova
documents.subtitle: Dominion Ghost
documents.text:
Nova's Terran units are improved versions of their regular Terran counterparts, with more HP and damage output. However, these come at a cost: her units are all charge-based and cannot be produced at will. This means that she will have to ensure she takes as few losses as possible during engagements. Her calldowns go a long way to achieving this goal.

Nova's difficulty of play is moderate. While simple micro can get players through a Brutal game, her playstyle rewards actively micromanaging units, liberal use of calldowns and an aggressive hero unit play.

documents.title: Stukov
documents.subtitle: Infested Admiral
documents.text:
Stukov has multiple playstyles available to him. His Infested units automatically rally to a Psi Emitter he places on the map, providing him with a "set it and forget it" style gameplay. As their lives are timed, they are usually routed to enemy bases and beat the enemy through attrition. He also has Infested Mechanical units, which are a key part of his more advanced gameplay.

Stukov is an easy commander to play, where even the most simple build can win missions. However, at high levels of play, he lacks in general power level compared to other commanders when it comes to efficiently dealing with certain attack wave types and general damage output.

documents.title: Fenix
documents.subtitle: Purifier Executor
documents.text:
Fenix has a very powerful hero unit as well as six other Protoss units that can be buffed by a Champion AI, giving them access to unique abilities. As such, Fenix's core gameplay requires him to build at least one of each of the units that support Champion AI's, which means he has to research both Ground and Air upgrades. Combined with the fact that Champion AI's get stronger when they have other similar non-buffed units around them, Fenix is usually forced to research all upgrades for many units, severely taxing his macro capabilities.

Given the macro-intensity of the commander, Fenix is still not a difficult commander to play. Simpler builds exist and are reasonably effective. At high skill level, Fenix's army is extremely powerful and can handle even the toughest of circumstances.

documents.title: Dehaka
documents.subtitle: Primal Pack Leader
documents.text:
Dehaka's Zerg units are all Primal variants of their regular counterparts. While they are more expensive, they are better in almost every way. Two Primal Zerg units can undergo Primal Combat to convert into a single evolved Primal Unit. However, Dehaka's greatest strength comes from his hero unit that levels up as he gathers Essence from fallen enemies. Combined with the buffs he gains based on Devoured enemy tags, his Hero unit can solo entire missions if played well, with only the support of his calldown Pack Leaders.

Dehaka is an easy commander to play but rewards intensive micro. Expert Dehaka players have no troubles dealing with the largest of attack waves by utilizing a high-leveled Dehaka to take engagements and debuff enemy units.

documents.title: Han & Horner
documents.subtitle: Mercenary Leader and Dominion Admiral
documents.text:
Han & Horner bring together two different types of Terran units: Ground units and Air units. While Mira's units are weak, they trigger various effects when they die, applying powerful buffs to the units in their vicinity. Horner's Air units are much stronger, but also much more expensive. While it is very difficult for players to take direct engagements, calldowns can be used to soften enemy forces and keeping their units alive.

Han & Horner may appear to be easy to play, but their units are fragile, which can be extremely punishing without adequate understanding of the co-op mechanics. However, with good map awareness, calldowns can be used to cover for the army's weaknesses.

documents.title: Tychus
documents.subtitle: Legendary Outlaw
documents.text:
Tychus is one of the more unique commanders in the game in the sense that he doesn't build an army of units. He plays with at most four other units in the game, referred to as "outlaws". Every few minutes, he gains the ability to recruit one more outlaw from a choice of eight outlaws, each having different abilities and having their own specialties.

Tychus is a very easy commander to play and extremely powerful, even at sub-mastery levels. His outlaws have a wide range of specialties that allow the player to pick and choose accordingly to handle the challenges they face.

documents.title: Zeratul
documents.subtitle: Dark Prelate
documents.text:
Zeratul gains upgrades as he collects Xel'Naga artifacts that are scattered around the map. Each artifact provides Zeratul with a choice of three calldowns and increases the attack, armor and shield upgrade of his units, as well as providing them with new passives and abilities. Zeratul hosts a wide variety of playstyles, all equally potent, regardless of player experience.

Zeratul is an easy commander to play, but one of the most powerful commanders in co-op. He is an extremely adaptable commander, making him very effective on all missions and mutations.

documents.title: Stetmann
documents.subtitle: Hero Genius (Henius)
documents.text:
Stetmann's core gameplay revolves around the use of his hero unit: Gary, which can be upgraded to Super Gary, unlocking more abilities and ability charges. Additionally, his Zerg units consume energy as they use their special abilities, which can be replenished by placing them in his Stetzones. Stetzones have three modes of operation: movement speed, healing, energy regeneration, which can be switched depending on the needs of the player.

Stetmann is a difficult commander to play that requires very aggressive use of Super Gary during the early game to carry his weight, as it takes a while for him to get units out at a certain power level. With aggressive Gary play and Stetzone spreads, Stetmann can control the map, making short work of most mission objectives.

documents.title: Mengsk
documents.subtitle: Emperor of the Dominion
documents.text:
Mengsk uses an army of cheap Conscripts and more valuable Royal Guard which level up as enemies are killed around the map to fight his battles for him. He also has Earthsplitter Ordnances which can shell enemy units from high range. Good macro is a critical part of Mengsk gameplay, especially in the early game.

Mengsk is a fairly simple commander to play, with a wide variety of playstyles that can keep missions fresh for players. Players that enjoy macro'ing will find Mengsk particularly enjoyable, due to the amount of depth the commander has.

documents.title: Afraid of the Dark
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Vision provided by all sources is extremely limited except when in view of your camera.

documents.title: Aggressive Deployment
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Additional enemy units are periodically deployed onto the battlefield.

documents.title: Alien Incubation
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: All enemy units spawn Broodlings upon death.

documents.title: Avenger
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units gain increased attack speed, movement speed, armor, life, and life-regeneration when nearby enemy units die.

documents.title: Barrier
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units and structures gain a temporary shield upon the first time they take damage.

documents.title: Black Death
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Some enemy units carry a plague that deals damage over time and spreads to other nearby units. The plague spreads to your units when the enemy unit is killed.

documents.title: Blizzard
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Storm clouds move across the map, damaging and freezing player units in their path.

documents.title: Chaos Studios
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Mutators are chosen at random and periodically cycle throughout the mission.

documents.title: Concussive Attacks
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Player units are slowed by all enemy attacks.

documents.title: Darkness
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Previously explored areas remain blacked out on the minimap while outside of player vision.

documents.title: Diffusion
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Damage dealt to enemies is split evenly across all nearby units, including your own.

documents.title: Double Edged
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Damage dealt by your units and structures is dealt back to them, but then healed back over time.

documents.title: Eminent Domain
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemies gain control of your structures after destroying them.

documents.title: Evasive Maneuvers
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units teleport a short distance away upon taking damage.

documents.title: Fatal Attraction
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: When enemy units and structures die, any of your nearby units are pulled to their location.

documents.title: Fear
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Player units will occasionally stop attacking and run around in fear upon taking damage.

documents.title: Fireworks
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemies launch a dazzling fireworks display upon death, dealing damage to your nearby units.

documents.title: Gift Exchange
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Gifts are periodically deployed around the map. If you don't claim them, Amon will!

documents.title: Going Nuclear
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Nukes are launched at random throughout the map.

documents.title: Hardened Will
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy Heroic units reduce all incoming damage to a maximum of 10 when any non-heroic enemy unit is near them.

documents.title: Heroes from the Storm
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Every attack wave will be led by Heroes of increasing power.

documents.title: Inspiration
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy Heroic units increase the attack speed and armor of all enemies within a small range.

documents.title: Just Die
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units are automatically revived upon death.

documents.title: Kill Bots
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Waves of bots attack players and are invulnerable to damage until they have killed a set amount of units.

documents.title: Laser Drill
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: An enemy Drakken laser drill constantly attacks player units within enemy vision.

documents.title: Lava Burst
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Lava periodically bursts from the ground at random locations and deals damage to player air and ground units.

documents.title: Life Leech
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units steal life or shields whenever they do damage.

documents.title: Long Range
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units and structures have increased weapon and vision range.

documents.title: Lucky Envelopes
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Festive envelopes containing resource pickups are dropped at random throughout the map.

documents.title: Mag-nificent
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Mag Mines are deployed throughout the map at the start of the mission.

documents.title: Micro Transactions
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Giving commands to your units costs resources based on the unit's cost.

documents.title: Mineral Shields
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Mineral clusters at player bases are periodically encased in a shield which must be destroyed for gathering to continue.

documents.title: Minesweeper
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Groups of Widow Mines and Spider Mines are buried throughout the battlefield.

documents.title: Missile Command
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Endless missile bombardments target your structures and must be shot down throughout the mission.

documents.title: Moment of Silence
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: When a Heroic enemy dies, all player units around it will reflect on their transgressions, leaving them temporarily unable to attack or use abilities.

documents.title: Mutually Assured Destruction
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy Hybrid units detonate a Nuke upon death.

documents.title: Naughty List
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Player units and structures take increased damage for each enemy they've killed.

documents.title: Orbital Strike
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy Orbital Strikes are periodically fired throughout the map.

documents.title: Outbreak
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Continuous waves of the infested horde march against the commanders' bases.

documents.title: Photon Overload
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: All enemy structures attack nearby hostile units.

documents.title: Polarity
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Each enemy unit is immune to either your units or your ally's units.

documents.title: Power Overwhelming
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: All enemy units have energy and use random abilities.

documents.title: Propagators
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Reality warping sludges are crawling towards you. Anything they touch is turned into a copy of the sludge.

documents.title: Purifier Beam
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: An enemy Purifier Beam moves across the map toward nearby player units.

documents.title: Scorched Earth
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units set the terrain on fire upon death.

documents.title: Self Destruction
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units explode and deal damage to friendly player units upon death.

documents.title: Sharing is Caring
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Supply is shared between you and your partner, and units from both armies contribute to your combined supply cap.

documents.title: Shortsighted
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Player units and structures have reduced vision range.

documents.title: Slim Pickings
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Player worker units gather resources at a reduced rate, but resource pickups spawn throughout the map.

documents.title: Speed Freaks
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: The enemy moves faster than any unit a player has at their disposal.

documents.title: Temporal Field
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy Temporal Fields are periodically deployed throughout the map.

documents.title: Time Warp
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy Time Warps are periodically deployed throughout the map.

documents.title: Transmutation
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Enemy units have a chance to transform into more powerful units whenever they deal damage.

documents.title: Trick or Treat
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Civilians visit your Candy Bowl looking for treats, which are generated by spending minerals. If no treats are available, the civilians randomly transform into enemy units.

documents.title: Turkey Shoot
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Supply can only be generated by killing turkeys that wander throughout the map. Doing so may anger the turkeys that remain.

documents.title: Twister
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Tornadoes move across the map, damaging and knocking back player units in their path.

documents.title: Void Reanimators
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Void Reanimators wander the battlefield, bringing your enemies back to life.

documents.title: Void Rifts
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Void Rifts periodically appear in random locations and spawn enemy units until destroyed.

documents.title: Walking Infested
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Every enemy unit that commanders destroy will spawn infested terrans. The more life a unit has, the more infested will rise from its corpse.

documents.title: We Move Unseen
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: All enemy units are permanently cloaked.

documents.title: Boom Bots
documents.subtitle: Mutator
documents.text: Uncaring automatons carry a nuclear payload toward your base. One player must discern the disarming sequence and the other player must enter it.

documents.title: Earthsplitter Ordnance

documents.text: Up to 4 Conscripts can be loaded into the structure, each increasing the number of volleys in 30 seconds by 1.

4.v:
Raynor has access to a mix of classical Terran Biological units (such as Marines and Marauders) and Mechanical Units (such as Siege Tanks and Vikings). He can drop units directly at their rally points. Combined with his potentially limitless access to MULEs, strong macro capabilities will be rewarded.

He is generally a difficult commander to play, and even harder to master. But at high levels of play, he is able to spawn-camp ground attack waves and sustain pushes indefinitely.

Kerrigan plays with classical Zerg units such as Zerglings, Hydralisks and Mutalisks. Her mobility is greatly improved by the availability of her Omega Worms. However, her greatest strength is the Kerrigan hero unit herself. Kerrigan has access to a wide range of abilities that provide the player with a strong early-game presence, while simultaneously offering them the capability of improving their economy.

Kerrigan's is an easy commander to play, but does require good micro of the hero unit to truly excel in games.

Artanis uses classic Protoss units as the main components of his army. While his army units themselves are fairly fragile, his Spear of Adun abilities compensate for his army's weaknesses by providing him with abilities that increase their survivability.

Artanis is a commander with simple mechanics, but a player will have to be attentive so as to keep his army alive. With adequate understanding of enemy compositions, a player can use Artanis' army to control entire attack waves, or weaken them using his Spear of Adun abilities.

Swann utilizes Terran Mechanical units such as Siege Tanks and Thors to build up his army. These units are backed up by his powerful Drakken Laser Drill, which can be upgraded to unlock additional abilities. Finally, he is also able to construct a wide variety of static defense, allowing him to control strategic positions on the map.

Swann is a very difficult commander to play, and requires a lot of micro to ensure his army remains effective. However, a veteran Swann player's army can be terrifyingly strong, shredding any attack wave that approaches it within seconds.

Zagara's standard army composition includes a mix of Zerglings, Baneling and Scourge. With the help of the Frenzy buff that Zagara's hero unit herself provides, a maximum supply army rarely struggles against any enemy composition or any enemy base. She is a hyper-aggressive commander, but will struggle on certain defensive missions.

Zagara is a fairly easy commander to play that favors a more macro-centric playstyle. While at low levels, Zagara is a very weak commander, at Ascension levels, she is one of the most powerful commanders available if played right, with a very strong early game presence.

Vorazun provides passive buffs to cloaked units that increase their shield and energy regeneration. Her army is comprised of high damage, low HP units, and thus, will require attention on the player's behalf. However, Vorazun dominates in terms of crowd control and disabling enemies with some of the most powerful abilities in co-op.

Vorazun is a simple commander to play. Players that have good map awareness and are efficient with their calldowns will have no problems handling anything thrown at them.

Karax specializes in the usage of Protoss Mechanical units and static defense. His playstyle requires good map awareness, efficient use of static defense, as well as careful usage of his Spear of Adun abilities.

Karax is one of the hardest commanders to play correctly. Significantly weak during sub-mastery and sub-ascension play, extra care must be taken so as to not lose his army while taking engagements.

Abathur's units, which consists of classic Zerg units such as Roaches and Mutalisks, collect Biomass from slain enemies. With enough Biomass, they evolve into powerful Ultimate Evolutions that can essentially complete missions without any further backup. His first Ultimate Evolution provides him with extreme early-game capabilities.

Abathur's difficulty depends on the initiative a player takes, generally rewarding aggressive play. Farming Biomass in the early game leads to a very straightforward late-game, including spawn-camping ground attack waves and complete map control.

Alarak's Tal'darim Ascendants have some of the highest damage output of any units in the game. However, these come at a cost of being extremely difficult to build up. As a result, Alarak relies on his Hero unit as well as his Photon Overload ability to provide him with the damage output to deal with the early stages of the game. He excels at dealing damage to units, but does struggle a bit when it comes to clearing structures.

Alarak is one of the most difficult commanders to play well. He is extremely punishing if a player takes a bad engagement, almost finding it impossible to recover. But with adequate understanding of his mechanics, Alarak players can decimate enemy waves and melt through attack waves while taking minimal losses.

Nova's Terran units are improved versions of their regular Terran counterparts, with more HP and damage output. However, these come at a cost: her units are all charge-based and cannot be produced at will. This means that she will have to ensure she takes as few losses as possible during engagements. Her calldowns go a long way to achieving this goal.

Nova's difficulty of play is moderate. While simple micro can get players through a Brutal game, her playstyle rewards actively micromanaging units, liberal use of calldowns and an aggressive hero unit play.

Stukov has multiple playstyles available to him. His Infested units automatically rally to a Psi Emitter he places on the map, providing him with a "set it and forget it" style gameplay. As their lives are timed, they are usually routed to enemy bases and beat the enemy through attrition. He also has Infested Mechanical units, which are a key part of his more advanced gameplay.

Stukov is an easy commander to play, where even the most simple build can win missions. However, at high levels of play, he lacks in general power level compared to other commanders when it comes to efficiently dealing with certain attack wave types and general damage output.

Fenix has a very powerful hero unit as well as six other Protoss units that can be buffed by a Champion AI, giving them access to unique abilities. As such, Fenix's core gameplay requires him to build at least one of each of the units that support Champion AI's, which means he has to research both Ground and Air upgrades. Combined with the fact that Champion AI's get stronger when they have other similar non-buffed units around them, Fenix is usually forced to research all upgrades for many units, severely taxing his macro capabilities.

Given the macro-intensity of the commander, Fenix is still not a difficult commander to play. Simpler builds exist and are reasonably effective. At high skill level, Fenix's army is extremely powerful and can handle even the toughest of circumstances.

Dehaka's Zerg units are all Primal variants of their regular counterparts. While they are more expensive, they are better in almost every way. Two Primal Zerg units can undergo Primal Combat to convert into a single evolved Primal Unit. However, Dehaka's greatest strength comes from his hero unit that levels up as he gathers Essence from fallen enemies. Combined with the buffs he gains based on Devoured enemy tags, his Hero unit can solo entire missions if played well, with only the support of his calldown Pack Leaders.

Dehaka is an easy commander to play but rewards intensive micro. Expert Dehaka players have no troubles dealing with the largest of attack waves by utilizing a high-leveled Dehaka to take engagements and debuff enemy units.

Han & Horner bring together two different types of Terran units: Ground units and Air units. While Mira's units are weak, they trigger various effects when they die, applying powerful buffs to the units in their vicinity. Horner's Air units are much stronger, but also much more expensive. While it is very difficult for players to take direct engagements, calldowns can be used to soften enemy forces and keeping their units alive.

Han & Horner may appear to be easy to play, but their units are fragile, which can be extremely punishing without adequate understanding of the co-op mechanics. However, with good map awareness, calldowns can be used to cover for the army's weaknesses.

Tychus is one of the more unique commanders in the game in the sense that he doesn't build an army of units. He plays with at most four other units in the game, referred to as "outlaws". Every few minutes, he gains the ability to recruit one more outlaw from a choice of eight outlaws, each having different abilities and having their own specialties.

Tychus is a very easy commander to play and extremely powerful, even at sub-mastery levels. His outlaws have a wide range of specialties that allow the player to pick and choose accordingly to handle the challenges they face.

Zeratul gains upgrades as he collects Xel'Naga artifacts that are scattered around the map. Each artifact provides Zeratul with a choice of three calldowns and increases the attack, armor and shield upgrade of his units, as well as providing them with new passives and abilities. Zeratul hosts a wide variety of playstyles, all equally potent, regardless of player experience.

Zeratul is an easy commander to play, but one of the most powerful commanders in co-op. He is an extremely adaptable commander, making him very effective on all missions and mutations.

Stetmann's core gameplay revolves around the use of his hero unit: Gary, which can be upgraded to Super Gary, unlocking more abilities and ability charges. Additionally, his Zerg units consume energy as they use their special abilities, which can be replenished by placing them in his Stetzones. Stetzones have three modes of operation: movement speed, healing, energy regeneration, which can be switched depending on the needs of the player.

Stetmann is a difficult commander to play that requires very aggressive use of Super Gary during the early game to carry his weight, as it takes a while for him to get units out at a certain power level. With aggressive Gary play and Stetzone spreads, Stetmann can control the map, making short work of most mission objectives.

Mengsk uses an army of cheap Conscripts and more valuable Royal Guard which level up as enemies are killed around the map to fight his battles for him. He also has Earthsplitter Ordnances which can shell enemy units from high range. Good macro is a critical part of Mengsk gameplay, especially in the early game.

Mengsk is a fairly simple commander to play, with a wide variety of playstyles that can keep missions fresh for players. Players that enjoy macro'ing will find Mengsk particularly enjoyable, due to the amount of depth the commander has.
Vision provided by all sources is extremely limited except when in view of your camera.
Additional enemy units are periodically deployed onto the battlefield.
All enemy units spawn Broodlings upon death.
Enemy units gain increased attack speed, movement speed, armor, life, and life-regeneration when nearby enemy units die.
Enemy units and structures gain a temporary shield upon the first time they take damage.
Some enemy units carry a plague that deals damage over time and spreads to other nearby units. The plague spreads to your units when the enemy unit is killed.
Storm clouds move across the map, damaging and freezing player units in their path.
Mutators are chosen at random and periodically cycle throughout the mission.
Player units are slowed by all enemy attacks.
Previously explored areas remain blacked out on the minimap while outside of player vision.
Damage dealt to enemies is split evenly across all nearby units, including your own.
Damage dealt by your units and structures is dealt back to them, but then healed back over time.
Enemies gain control of your structures after destroying them.
Enemy units teleport a short distance away upon taking damage.
When enemy units and structures die, any of your nearby units are pulled to their location.
Player units will occasionally stop attacking and run around in fear upon taking damage.
Enemies launch a dazzling fireworks display upon death, dealing damage to your nearby units.
Gifts are periodically deployed around the map. If you don't claim them, Amon will!
Nukes are launched at random throughout the map.
Enemy Heroic units reduce all incoming damage to a maximum of 10 when any non-heroic enemy unit is near them.
Every attack wave will be led by Heroes of increasing power.
Enemy Heroic units increase the attack speed and armor of all enemies within a small range.
Enemy units are automatically revived upon death.
Waves of bots attack players and are invulnerable to damage until they have killed a set amount of units.
An enemy Drakken laser drill constantly attacks player units within enemy vision.
Lava periodically bursts from the ground at random locations and deals damage to player air and ground units.
Enemy units steal life or shields whenever they do damage.
Enemy units and structures have increased weapon and vision range.
Festive envelopes containing resource pickups are dropped at random throughout the map.
Mag Mines are deployed throughout the map at the start of the mission.
Giving commands to your units costs resources based on the unit's cost.
Mineral clusters at player bases are periodically encased in a shield which must be destroyed for gathering to continue.
Groups of Widow Mines and Spider Mines are buried throughout the battlefield.
Endless missile bombardments target your structures and must be shot down throughout the mission.
When a Heroic enemy dies, all player units around it will reflect on their transgressions, leaving them temporarily unable to attack or use abilities.
Enemy Hybrid units detonate a Nuke upon death.
Player units and structures take increased damage for each enemy they've killed.
Enemy Orbital Strikes are periodically fired throughout the map.
Continuous waves of the infested horde march against the commanders' bases.
All enemy structures attack nearby hostile units.
Each enemy unit is immune to either your units or your ally's units.
All enemy units have energy and use random abilities.
Reality warping sludges are crawling towards you. Anything they touch is turned into a copy of the sludge.
An enemy Purifier Beam moves across the map toward nearby player units.
Enemy units set the terrain on fire upon death.
Enemy units explode and deal damage to friendly player units upon death.
Supply is shared between you and your partner, and units from both armies contribute to your combined supply cap.
Player units and structures have reduced vision range.
Player worker units gather resources at a reduced rate, but resource pickups spawn throughout the map.
The enemy moves faster than any unit a player has at their disposal.
Enemy Temporal Fields are periodically deployed throughout the map.
Enemy Time Warps are periodically deployed throughout the map.
Enemy units have a chance to transform into more powerful units whenever they deal damage.
Civilians visit your Candy Bowl looking for treats, which are generated by spending minerals. If no treats are available, the civilians randomly transform into enemy units.
Supply can only be generated by killing turkeys that wander throughout the map. Doing so may anger the turkeys that remain.
Tornadoes move across the map, damaging and knocking back player units in their path.
Void Reanimators wander the battlefield, bringing your enemies back to life.
Void Rifts periodically appear in random locations and spawn enemy units until destroyed.
Every enemy unit that commanders destroy will spawn infested terrans. The more life a unit has, the more infested will rise from its corpse.
All enemy units are permanently cloaked.
Uncaring automatons carry a nuclear payload toward your base. One player must discern the disarming sequence and the other player must enter it.
